author: t5k6
description: Imports highlights and metadata from KoReader
name: KOReader Highlight Importer

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New
- Added **Restore note from backup**.
- You can now restore a highlight note (available for files within your configured **highlights folder**) from:
  - the file context menu (**Restore KOReader backup**),
  - the editor menu, or
  - Command Palette (**KOReader: Restore note from backup**).
- Added a restore dialog with:
  - backup selection list,
  - content preview,
  - overwrite confirmation.
### Improved
- Backup handling is smarter: duplicate backups are skipped when note content has not changed.
- Backup matching and cleanup are more reliable, including per-note retention behavior.
- Import completion messages now mention that backups are available and how to restore them.
- **Template rendering whitespace handling.** `templateCore` now trims leading/trailing render artifacts and skips empty output from conditional/empty variables, which reduces extra blank lines in generated notes while preserving meaningful paragraph breaks.
- **Default template cleanup.** `templates/default.md` was tightened to remove unnecessary blank spacer lines around page/date, highlight, and optional note blocks so output is cleaner out of the box.

### Fixed
- **Duplicate highlights on import.** Some `.sdr` metadata files list the same highlights twice: once under the modern `annotations` table and again under the legacy page-indexed `highlight` table. The importer now keeps a single copy per highlight (matched by document position), so notes no longer show each quote twice.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.4.4...1.4.5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release for version 1.4.4

## What's Changed
* fix: support non-ASCII characters in book matching keys by @ProudBenzene in https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/pull/30
* feat: add time/random vars, dateFormat filter and fix if-syntax by @ProudBenzene in https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/pull/31
Template system additions:
  - New variables: `{{time}}`, `{{randomHex}}`
  - New filters: `dateFormat`, `stripNewlines`, `singleLine`
  - `{{#if var}}...{{/if}}` conditional syntax supported
* Optimize the scanning book list feature to distinguish books with blank annotations by @ProudBenzene in https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/pull/32
Scan report now categorizes results into:
  - Files with highlights
  - Files without highlights
  - Errors (unreadable or invalid files)


- Note quoting mode setting: `auto`, `manual`, `never` (#29)

## Notes for Users

- If you previously imported books with non-ASCII titles/authors, you may see temporary duplicates after updating due to improved matching. Review and merge as needed.

## New Contributors
* @ProudBenzene made their first contribution in https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/pull/30

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.4.3...1.4.4

Release for version 1.4.3

## New Features

- **Silent Import Mode**  ([#28](https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/issues/28)): 
Enable automatic imports without merge dialogs - perfect for scheduled imports with Syncthing or similar tools
  - Configure in Settings → Formatting section
  - Choose default merge strategy (Merge/Replace/Skip)
  - Ideal for automated workflows and mobile syncing setups
  
## Bug Fix

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.4.2...1.4.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Notes - New Features & Stability

## New Feature: Keywords to Tags
You can now automatically convert or duplicate your book's keywords into Obsidian tags. This allows for better searching and grouping within your vault.

- **Flexible Handling**: Choose to keep keywords as links, duplicate them as tags, or replace links with tags entirely.
- **Smart Sanitization**: Tags are automatically cleaned for Obsidian compatibility (e.g., "Health & Fitness" becomes `#Health-and-Fitness`).
- **Where to find it**: Settings > Formatting > "Convert Keywords to Tags".

## Improvements & Fixes

### Better Settings Experience
- **Smooth Input**: Fixed an issue where "Excluded folders" and "Allowed file types" would reset while you were typing.
- **Native Look**: The settings interface now matches the standard Obsidian look and feel more closely.
- **Responsive Feedback**: Improved speed and visual feedback when switching templates or options.

### Enhanced Reliability
- **Accurate Page Labels**: Added support for KOReader's "Physical Page References". Your notes can now show page numbers matching the print edition (e.g., "p. 42") instead of just the internal digital count. *Thanks to **@alva-seal** for the suggestion!*
- **Cleaner Highlights**: Special characters (like `*` or `_`) inside your highlights are now automatically escaped, preventing them from accidentally breaking your note's formatting.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.4.1...1.4.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Release Notes (v1.4.1)

**Highlights**
This release focuses on long-term stability, fixing a critical data bloat issue, and providing new tools to help you manage your library's hygiene.

**Critical Fixes**
*   **Fixed Recursive Settings Bloat (Issue #25):** Fixed a bug where the plugin's settings file (`data.json`) would nest itself recursively on every save, eventually becoming megabytes in size.
    *   *Note:* The plugin will automatically repair your settings file on the next startup.
*   **Readable Punctuation:** Fixed an issue where apostrophes and quotes in highlights were converted to HTML entities (e.g., `book's` becoming `book's`). Your highlights now remain readable in raw Markdown.

**New Features & Tools**
*   **"Finalize Note" Command:** You can now right-click a note (Provided the note is in your Highlights Folder) and select **"Finalize KOReader note"**. This removes the hidden tracking metadata (`%% KOHL ... %%`), detaching the note from sync updates and leaving you with clean Markdown.
*   **Orphaned Data Cleanup:** Added a maintenance command to delete "orphaned" snapshots (backup files used for merging that no longer have a corresponding note in your vault).

**Improvements**
*   **Cleaner Frontmatter:** Metadata fields in your notes (YAML) are now consistently sorted and formatted. This prevents fields from jumping around randomly during updates and keeps your file history clean.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.3.0...1.4.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## **Changelog: KOReader Highlights v1.4.0**

Core components have been refactored for better long-term maintainability and to resolve several long-standing issues.

### ⚠️ **CRITICAL: ACTION REQUIRED FOR ALL USERS**

This release fixes a critical bug where notes with special characters (e.g., colons, commas, wikilinks) in the title or author were duplicated on every import.

To apply this fix and prevent future duplicates, you are advised to **reset the plugin's internal index** after updating. This is a one-time, safe operation that **will not affect your notes**.

1.  Update the KOReader Highlights plugin to **v1.4.0**.
2.  Open the Command Palette (`Ctrl/Cmd + P`).
3.  Run the command: **`Troubleshoot: Full Reset and Reload Plugin`**.

The plugin will reload and automatically rebuild its index in the background.

---

### ✨ **New Features & Major Improvements**

*   **Live Template Editor:**
    *   A new "Preview" button in the template settings opens a split-screen modal for real-time editing and previewing of custom templates.
    *   You can now view the source code of built-in templates.
    *   Changes can be saved or reverted directly from the editor.

*   **Improved Device & Path Handling:**
    *   The "KOReader Data Folder" setting now provides instant validation feedback on the selected path.
    *   Device scanning performance has been improved for faster discovery of highlight files.

*   **More Robust Duplicate Detection:**
    *   The duplicate scanning process is now stream-based, improving UI responsiveness during large scans.
    *   Scans on very large vaults now time out more gracefully, with clearer user feedback, preventing UI freezes.

### 🎨 **UI & User Experience**

*   **UI Consistency:** Replaced multiple different pop-up dialogs with a single, consistent modal for all user interactions.
*   **Better Folder Suggester:** The folder selection dropdown in settings is now faster and more responsive.
*   **Richer Progress Indicators:** The progress modal now supports indeterminate states for tasks of unknown duration.
*   **Settings Reorganized:** The "Formatting" and "Filtering & Exclusion" sections have been merged for clarity.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.3.0...1.4.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## **KOReader Highlight Importer - Version 1.3.0 Release Notes**

This release focuses on significant improvements to the plugin's reliability, speed, and how it manages your imported notes. Version 1.3.0 includes a major internal rewrite to enhance stability and ensure data integrity.

**Important: Automatic Data Migrations**
Upon updating to 1.3.0, the plugin will perform several automatic updates to its internal data. This process is non-destructive and will not alter or delete your existing highlight notes or plugin settings. These migrations are necessary for the new features and reliability improvements. You may see a temporary "Building temporary index..." notice in your status bar during this process.

---

### **Key Improvements in Version 1.3.0**

1.  **Enhanced Data Management for Your Notes:**
    *   **Permanent Note IDs:** Each imported highlight note now receives a unique ID in its frontmatter. This allows the plugin to track your notes accurately, even if you rename or move them within your vault.
    *   **Reliable Note Merging:** When re-importing highlights for a note you've already edited, the plugin now performs a 3-way merge. It combines your existing edits with new highlights from your device, aiming to preserve your changes.
    *   **Clear Conflict Handling:** If the merge process detects conflicting changes (e.g., on the same lines of text), these conflicts will be marked directly in your note using Obsidian callouts, allowing you to resolve them manually.
    *   **Consistent Data State:** Internal operations are designed to maintain data consistency, reducing the risk of corruption during critical actions.

2.  **Improved Performance and Broader Compatibility:**
    *   **Faster Duplicate Detection:** A new internal SQLite database accelerates the process of checking for existing notes, resulting in faster import times, particularly for large libraries.
    *   **Resilient Indexing:** If the plugin's persistent index database is inaccessible (e.g., due to file permissions or cloud sync issues), it will automatically switch to a temporary in-memory index for the session. A background process will then rebuild this index to maintain performance.
    *   **More Robust File Operations:** Enhanced retry mechanisms for file system interactions make the plugin more tolerant of transient issues that can occur during reads and writes.

3.  **User Interface and Control:**
    *   **Updated Settings Interface:** The settings tab has been reorganized for improved clarity and ease of use.
    *   **Clearer Dialogs:** Prompts for handling duplicate notes and notes located in unexpected folders now provide more detailed information and clearer options.
    *   **"Refresh highlights for this book" Command:** A new command, accessible from the command palette or a status bar icon (when active on a highlight note), allows you to re-import highlights specifically for the current note.
    *   **"Convert All Files to Current Comment Style" Command:** This new command helps standardize the comment style (HTML, Markdown, or None) across all your existing highlight notes.
    *   **"Troubleshoot: Full Reset and Reload Plugin" Command:** A new option to clear all plugin data (index, caches, backups) and reload the plugin, useful for addressing persistent issues. Your highlight notes are not deleted by this action.
    *   **"Troubleshoot: Re-check environment capabilities" Command:** Provides immediate feedback on plugin permissions and folder accessibility.

---

### **Summary of Changes**

*   **Data Integrity:** Implemented unique note IDs (`kohl-uid`) and robust 3-way merging with explicit conflict marking.
*   **Performance:** Introduced a SQLite-backed index for faster duplicate checks.
*   **Resilience:** Added in-memory index fallback and improved file operation retry logic.
*   **User Interface:** Redesigned settings, clearer modals, and new commands for managing imports and notes.
*   **Settings:** Introduced `KOReader Scan Path` (migrates from old `KOReader Mount Point`) and `Backup Retention Days`.
*   **Internal:** Major architectural refactoring for improved maintainability and testability.

---

Thank you for using the KOReader Highlight Importer. We encourage you to report any issues or provide feedback.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.2.2...1.3.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### **Release Highlights: Core Engine Refactor and Reliability Improvements**

This release introduces significant architectural improvements to the plugin The core engine for file handling, data indexing, and concurrency has been refactored to improve performance, data consistency, and reliability.

#### **Performance and Efficiency**

*   **New Import Index:** The plugin now uses a persistent index (`import-index.json`) to track the state (`mtime`, `size`) of KOReader `metadata.lua` files. Subsequent imports will now skip any source file that has not changed, resulting in a substantial performance increase for users with large libraries.
*   **Optimized Concurrency:** Manual file-locking has been replaced with a lightweight, key-based promise queue. This improves memory management and provides more efficient serialization for filesystem operations without the risk of race conditions.

#### **Data Integrity and Robustness**

*   **Improved Duplicate Detection in Degraded Mode:** If the persistent SQLite index is unavailable (e.g., due to filesystem restrictions), the plugin now builds a temporary in-memory index in the background. This ensures that duplicate detection remains functional without blocking the UI.
*   **Interactive Timeout Handling:** If a duplicate scan times out, the plugin will now prompt the user for an action (e.g., "Import Anyway" or "Skip") instead of potentially creating a duplicate note. The timeout duration is now also configurable in the settings.
*   **Robust Filesystem Operations:** The `FileSystemService` has been hardened. It now uses Obsidian's adapter for all vault-internal I/O, preventing path-related errors. Capability checks for snapshotting and backups are now more reliable, preventing operations that cannot be safely completed.

#### **Other Improvements**

*   **Centralized Database Management:** A new `SqlJsManager` now handles the lifecycle of all SQLite databases, improving consistency in how they are opened, validated, and persisted.
*   **Refined Frontmatter Merging:** The logic for merging frontmatter has been updated with explicit field-level policies, preventing unintended data removal from a note if the source data is temporarily incomplete.
*   **Clearer Error Reporting:** Error handling has been standardized to provide more specific feedback and user-friendly notices for common issues.

## New Contributors
* @OptionalM made their first contribution in https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/pull/16

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.2.0...1.2.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release for version 1.2.1

- Added new date formats for templates for better date representation:
    -`{{date}}` The date the highlight was made, in a stable `en-US` format (e.g., `Jan 1, 2024`).
    -`{{localeDate}}` The date formatted according to your computer's local settings (e.g., `1. Jan. 2024` or `1/1/2024`).
    -`{{dailyNoteLink}}` An Obsidian wikilink to the daily note for that date (e.g., `[[2024-01-01]]`). 
- Improved highlight merging logic to handle paragraph breaks and separators more effectively.
- Introduced CacheManager to centralize cache creation and management, replacing direct LruCache usage in multiple services.
- Added PromptModal for user input in TemplateSettingsSection, allowing creation of custom templates from built-in ones.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.2.0...1.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release for version 1.2.0

## What's Changed

**🎯 Better User Experience:**
- Improved settings interface with clearer organization and better readability
- Enhanced folder selection with real-time updates and automatic folder creation
- Faster and more reliable importing with built-in performance optimizations
- Use proper spelling of KOReader and consolidate folder structure by @harmtemolder in https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/pull/11

**📚 Enhanced Highlight Processing:**
- Better highlight extraction for more accurate content
- Improved duplicate detection and merging of highlights from the same book
- Enhanced template rendering with visual grouping and better formatting
- Smarter filename generation for your imported highlight files

**🔧 Performance & Reliability:**
- Added concurrency controls to prevent system overload during large imports
- Improved error handling and recovery during the import process
- Better logging for troubleshooting import issues
- Optimized file scanning and processing for faster imports

## New Contributors
* @harmtemolder made their first contribution in https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/pull/11

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/t5k6/obsidian-koreader-highlights/compare/1.1.1...1.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Refactor and enhance KoReader Highlights Importer Plugin for better usability and features

- **src/main.ts:**
  - Improved error handling and user notifications for better feedback.
  - Added sorting of highlights by page number in `generateHighlightsContent`.

- **src/parser.ts:**
  - Fixed potential issues in `extractModernAnnotation` and `extractLegacyAnnotations`.

- **src/settings.ts:**
  - Added Obsidian native imports for better folder handling and user experience.
  - Organized settings into sections (Core Settings, Filtering/Exclusion Settings, Actions, Advanced/Troubleshooting).
  - Added `Debug Mode` toggle to enable or disable debug logging.

- **src/types.ts:**
  - Improved type definitions.
